questions.hint: Differentiate each term separately using standard trigonometric and exponential rules.
Apply the reverse power rule and don't forget the constant of integration.

questions.text: Find the equation of the tangent line to the curve \(y = x^3 - 2x^2 + 4\) at the point where \(x = 2\).
questions.hint: First, find the derivative \(\frac{dy}{dx}\) to determine the gradient function. Substitute \(x = 2\) to find the specific gradient and the corresponding \(y\)-value, then use the point-slope form to write the equation.
